Cardi B showing up for Stefon Diggs’ charity event has fans saying the breakup may be over



So… are they back or nah?



Okay so remember when we were all shook hearing that Cardi B and Stefon Diggs called it quits back in February? Yeah, we cried a little too. The split hit different because these two had JUST welcomed their baby boy, Baby Brim, in November 2025. Like, the timeline was messy, the rumors were messier, and honestly we were not ready.But fast forward to now, and DC is out here spilling ALL the tea — and it's hot.Cardi recently touched down in the nation's capital, and guess who was right there in the mix? Stefon Diggs. His mom Stephanie. His brother, Trevon. The whole Diggs family showing UP and showing OUT. Now look, you don't bring your mama somewhere if things are fully dead. That's just not how it works. Mama Stephanie has literally been attending Cardi's tour stops; she was at the Houston show, too. At this point, Stephanie might be the biggest Cardi fan in the Diggs household and we are NOT complaining.And then Cardi and Stefon were spotted leaving together afterward. Together. As in, same exit, same direction, same vibe. Say less.What makes this DC outing even more meaningful is the cause behind it.Stefon's non-profit, the Diggs Deep Foundation, has been doing real work, hosting wellness events for mothers, giving back to communities. With Mother's Day right around the corner, Cardi being present for something this personal? That's not just a coincidence. That's a statement.You don't show up for a man's charity work if you're fully done with him. Just saying.Here's where we land: nothing is officially confirmed, no Instagram post with matching outfits (yet), no "we're back" announcement. But the signs are literally everywhere. The DC link-up, the foundation support, the fact that Stefon is from Gaithersburg, Maryland, just a stone's throw from DC, this all feels very intentional.Cardi once said on stage that she had a "Maryland n***a" who used to put her on her back. Girl was NOT being subtle. And neither are we.We're calling it, Cardi and Stefon are in their quiet reconciliation era and DC just gave us the first real receipt. Keep watching this space because something is coming.
Article Summary: Cardi B and Stefon Diggs were spotted together at a charity event in Washington, D.C. The Diggs Deep Foundation is Stefon's non-profit. Cardi was there with his mom Stephanie and his brother Trevon. The two were seen leaving together.

Article Title: ESPN reporter Laura Rutledge confesses initial disappointment over pregnancy news but now embraces motherhood
Article Text:
ESPN reporter Laura Rutledge confesses initial disappointment over pregnancy news but now embraces motherhood (Image via Getty: Laura Rutledge)

Laura Rutledge opens up about pregnancy, motherhood , and ESPN career

Laura Rutledge husband is a former MLB player

Mother’s Day is on the second Sunday of every May. This time, it is on the 10th of May. To honor the event, former NFL player Ryan Clark called ESPN reporter Laura Rutledge on his The Pivot podcast.Laura Rutledge talks about going from initial disappointment upon hearing pregnancy news to shedding tears of joy --- explaining how much she cherishes hearing her daughter, Reese Katherine Rutledge, and son, Jack Alexander Rutledge, say “Happy Mother’s Day.”For the longest time, Laura Rutledge thought motherhood would affect her ESPN career. She had been married for seven years, and while pursuing her dream career in 2019, she found out she was pregnant. Laura admitted she felt initially disappointed because she feared it would end her career. She didn’t think she could appear on TV while pregnant.Even during her pregnancy, Laura did everything she could career-wise, believing the baby’s arrival would prevent her from working at that level afterward. Fast forward to now: her most significant career milestones have actually happened after the birth of her two precious children.While Laura feels it’s a constant battle to juggle motherhood and a career --- and admits that at times there is no balance --- she uses those down days to remind herself how lucky she is to be a mother. Knowing her children will always say, “That’s my mom,” makes her proud of a life she once thought was impossible.When she was contemplating pregnancy before these two were born, she considered adoption because it wasn’t working out, but then she was blessed with these two amazing kids. She calls them her world. They are her everything.They changed her perspective, “How could I ever think that career was more important than what this is?And knowing that feeling of getting to experience them and being their comfort, and all of those things. It is the greatest joy of my life to celebrate Mother’s Day and to hear their little voices say --- Happy Mother’s Day.There is nothing more special than that. So, I’m so thankful. And I think that is it. It’s a day of gratitude, really. I’m mad at you for making me cry,” Rutledge said with tears of joy in her eyes.Laura McKeeman [now Rutledge] met her husband, Josh Rutledge, a former professional baseball player, in 2011. They got married in December 2013.They welcomed their firstborn, daughter Reese Katherine Rutledge, in October 2019, and their second-born and first son, Jack Alexander Rutledge, in May 2023. Reese is 6 years old, and Jack is almost 3.Laura Rutledge is a prominent reporter and host for the ESPN and SEC Network. She is a former Miss Florida 2012.According to his public LinkedIn profile, Josh Rutledge served as a real estate agent at Coldwell Banker in West Hartford, Connecticut until March 2026. He has owned West Alabama Ford in Sulligent since September 2022 and West Alabama Ford of Fayette since September 2025.
Article Summary: Laura Rutledge is a prominent reporter and host for the ESPN and SEC Network. She met her husband, Josh Rutledge, a former professional baseball player, in 2011. They welcomed their firstborn, Reese Katherine Rutledge,. in October 2019, and their second-born and first son, Jack Alexander Rutledge. in May 2023. Reese is 6 years old and Jack is almost 3.

Article Title: Patrick Mahomes’ wife Brittany gives honest insights into life with her three children Sterling, Bronze, and Golden
Article Text:
Patrick Mahomes’ wife Brittany gives honest insights into life with her three children Sterling, Bronze, and Golden (Image via X: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes)

Mother’s Day special treat: Brittany Mahomes chooses which kid does what

to help mom with anything

to try new foods

to listen to mom and dad the first time

to love a photoshoot

to become a soccer star

to share with their siblings

laugh at your jokes

to name all the

NFL

teams

to eat their meal the fastest

to make a funny face in a photo

to represent KC Current

Brittany Mahomes diagnosed with De Quervain’s tenosynovitis referred as Mother’s Wrist or Mommy Thumb

You know that social media trend where parents are questioned about specifics, and they have to choose which kid does what in particular?Well, that trend has reached Brittany Mahomes and her three little adorable cuties: Sterling, Bronze, and Golden.On the gridiron for Kansas City Current soccer team, the wife of Patrick Mahomes , Brittany Mahomes, was asked teeny-tiny questions about her three little precious babies. Starting with, “Which kid is most likely?” “Sterling,” the Mama said.Which kid is most likely? “Golden.” Which kid is most likely to help with steel and silver? “Sterling.”Which kid is most likely? “None of them,” Brittany said and chuckled. “The second time?” “Sterling, probably.” Looks like Sterling is the winner of this quiz. Wait, there are some more! Which kid is most likely? “Bronze.” Now there’s a pretty poser.The next question is short for: who will follow the mother’s footsteps? Which kid is most likely? “Sterling, right here” - without a doubt, said the mom of three. The reporter asked, “KC Current?” Brittany chucked and said, “Yep! KC Current right here!”A little parenting test: the next question is, “Which kid is most likely?” Bronze is the winner. Which kid is most likely to? Bronze is again the winner!Which kid is most likely? Now, this is a social media trend… several fans are testing their little babies with all 32 names! The NFL’s official Instagram account even shared a clip of the same: a toddler who could name all 32 teams when shown any one of the 32 teams’ tiny, cute little helmets! What a way to spend the offseason…Anyhow, Mrs. Mahomes said, “Bronze!” It looks like Sterling will enter soccer, and Bronze will follow the legendary quarterback Patrick Mahomes’ unfollowable footsteps… “He knows, he knows his stuff,” hinted Brittany.Which kid is most likely? “Sterling.” Which kid is most likely? “Sterling.” Which kid is most likely? “All of them!” What a fun way to end the quiz.While the mother knew every little detail about her three precious little cuties, something related to her went amiss for over a year…Patrick Mahomes’ wife, Brittany, has been suffering from a troubling wrist issue for over a year. The issue was ignored for a year. It worsened after a workout earlier in the week, which pushed her to address the issue.She was diagnosed with De Quervain’s tenosynovitis, referred to as “Mother’s Wrist” or “Mommy Thumb.” The causative factors are constantly lifting infants or kids and hormonal shifts, which can put pressure on the wrists.“To find out I have this… been battling it for probably around a year now, thinking it will get better, well, it never did and started to get pretty bad… so now, I get to wear this cool thing,” 30-year-old Brittany said via social media post showing her wrist brace.While she recovers, her star quarterback husband is also recovering from a brutal knee injury he encountered on the gridiron in December last year. Now we are all hoping for his Week 1 return…let’s see what happens.
Article Summary: Patrick Mahomes’ wife, Brittany, has been suffering from a troubling wrist issue for over a year. She was diagnosed with De Quervain’s tenosynovitis, referred to as “Mother's Wrist” or “Mommy Thumb”
